questions.text: A desert is an arid region characterized by:
questions.explanation: Deserts receive very little rain and have extreme temperatures.

questions.text: The world's largest hot desert is the:
questions.explanation: The Sahara covers a large part of North Africa.

questions.text: The Sahara desert touches how many countries?
questions.explanation: It covers Algeria, Chad, Egypt, Libya, Mali, Mauritania, Morocco, Niger, Sudan, Tunisia, and Western Sahara.

questions.text: The climate of the Sahara desert is:
questions.explanation: It has a short rainy season and temperatures can soar as high as 50°C during the day.

questions.text: During the night, temperatures in the Sahara can fall to nearly:
questions.explanation: The lack of moisture in the air causes the sand to lose heat rapidly at night.

questions.text: Vegetation in the Sahara includes:
questions.explanation: These plants are adapted to survive with very little water.

questions.text: A green island with water and trees in a desert is called a/an:
questions.explanation: Oases are formed when groundwater reaches the surface in depressions.

questions.text: Which of these animals is NOT common in the Sahara?
questions.explanation: Polar bears live in the Arctic regions, not in hot deserts.

questions.text: The Bedouins and Tuaregs are:
questions.explanation: They are nomadic groups who rear livestock for milk, leather, and hair.

questions.text: The discovery of ________ is constantly transforming the landscape of the Sahara.
questions.explanation: Oil in Algeria, Libya, and Egypt has significantly changed the region's economy.

questions.text: Ladakh is a ________ desert in the Himalayas.
questions.explanation: It is located in the Great Himalayas on the eastern side of Jammu and Kashmir.

questions.text: Ladakh is also known as 'Khapa-chan', which means:
questions.explanation: The term refers to the vast, snow-covered landscape of the region.

questions.text: The altitude in Ladakh varies from about 3,000 m in Kargil to more than ________ m in Karakoram.
questions.explanation: The high altitude makes the air so thin that the sun's heat can be felt intensely.

questions.text: The main river flowing through Ladakh is the:
questions.explanation: The Indus and its tributaries are the lifelines of this cold desert.

questions.text: The climate of Ladakh is extremely:
questions.explanation: Due to high altitude and being in the rain shadow of the Himalayas, it receives very little rain.

questions.text: Night temperatures in Ladakh in winter can fall below:
questions.explanation: Winters are severely cold, and the region remains frozen for several months.

questions.text: Which of these birds are common in Ladakh?
questions.explanation: Many of these birds are migratory birds seen during the summer.

questions.text: The 'Chiru' or the Tibetan Antelope is hunted for its wool known as:
questions.explanation: Shahtoosh wool is extremely light and very warm.

questions.text: The people of Ladakh are primarily:
questions.explanation: Ladakh has several famous Buddhist monasteries like Hemis and Thiksey.

questions.text: Buddhist monasteries in Ladakh are known as:
questions.explanation: Gompas are traditional centers of learning and worship for Buddhists.

questions.text: In summer, people of Ladakh are busy cultivating:
questions.explanation: Agriculture is possible only during the short summer when ice melts.

questions.text: The capital of Ladakh is:
questions.explanation: Leh is well connected by road and air to the rest of the country.

questions.text: The National Highway 1A connects Leh to Kashmir Valley through the ________ pass.
questions.explanation: This pass is a crucial link through the high mountain ranges.

questions.text: Tourism is a major activity in Ladakh because of:
options: Treks to see meadows and glaciers
questions.explanation: The unique culture and natural beauty attract tourists from all over the world.

questions.text: Due to scarcity of resources, the people of Ladakh have learned to live in ________ with nature.
questions.explanation: They are very careful with resources like water and fuel and waste nothing.

questions.text: Hemis, Thiksey, Shey and Lamayuru are famous ________ in Ladakh.
questions.explanation: These monasteries are significant cultural landmarks of Ladakh.

questions.text: The Tafilalet Oasis in Morocco is a huge oasis covering ________ sq. km.
questions.explanation: It is one of the largest and most famous oases in the Sahara.

questions.text: Which of these crops is famous in Egypt and grown near the Nile?
questions.explanation: Egyptian cotton is world-famous for its high quality.

questions.text: The wood of which tree in Ladakh is used to make the finest cricket bats?
questions.explanation: Willow trees grow in the valleys of Ladakh and Kashmir.

questions.text: Which animal provides milk, meat, and hair for making tents in the Sahara?
questions.explanation: Nomadic tribes depend heavily on these livestock for their survival.

questions.text: The Sahara once used to be a ________ plain.
questions.explanation: Cave paintings in the Sahara show that it was once a fertile region with rivers and wildlife.

questions.text: Drass, one of the coldest inhabited places on earth, is located in:
questions.explanation: Drass often records some of the lowest temperatures in the Indian subcontinent.

questions.text: Which glacier is found in Ladakh?
questions.explanation: Several glaciers, such as the Gangri glacier, are found in the mountain ranges of Ladakh.

questions.text: Deserts can be either ________ or ________ depending on the temperature.
questions.explanation: Deserts are categorized by their extreme temperature conditions.

questions.text: The 'Yak' is a domesticated animal in Ladakh used for:
questions.explanation: The Yak is extremely important to the economy and daily life of Ladakhis.

questions.text: Life is difficult in deserts because of the:
options: Shortage of water and extreme climate
questions.explanation: Finding water and managing extreme heat or cold are the primary challenges.

questions.text: Nomads in the Sahara use ________ for clothing as protection against dust storms.
questions.explanation: Heavy robes protect them from the sun, heat, and blowing sand.

questions.text: The Manali-Leh highway crosses four passes. Which of these is NOT one of them?
questions.explanation: Nathu La is in Sikkim, whereas the others are on the Manali-Leh route.

questions.text: In Ladakh, the summer season begins in:
questions.explanation: May to August is the time when the region is accessible and agriculture is possible.

questions.text: Which mineral is found in the Sahara in addition to oil?
questions.explanation: The Sahara is rich in several industrial minerals.
